I take a slow breath and stare at our linked hands. “So much of this has been pretend. From the very start, we’ve been lying to the public.” “I’ll give you a divorce.” That stops me short. I lift my head and blink at him. “What?” “A divorce.” The winter night outside the window is warmer than Eros’s voice. “You married me to keep safe from my mother. She’s no longer a threat, and I know this isn’t what you would have chosen for yourself. When the month is up, I’ll get the divorce papers written up. You can have whatever you want. But that’s not a good enough reason to keep you chained to me. I’m a selfish bastard and I thought I could do it, but I can’t bear the thought of you trapped. Not even by me.” I close my eyes.
